item.title: After sale of its shoe business, Allbirds pivots to AI
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Allbirds is ditching wool sneakers for AI servers, rebranding as NewBird AI after locking in a $50M convertible financing facility.

item.title: Sweden blames Russian hackers for attempting ‘destructive’ cyberattack on thermal plant
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Sweden's minister for civil defense said Russian hackers are "now attempting destructive cyber attacks against organizations in Europe."

item.title: Snap is cutting 1,000 jobs, 16% of its workforce
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The company cites advancements in AI for the cuts.

item.title: Reid Hoffman weighs in on the ‘tokenmaxxing’ debate
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Reid Hoffman says tracking AI token use can gauge adoption, but cautions it should be paired with context and not treated as a direct productivity metric.

item.title: Adobe’s new Firefly AI assistant can use Creative Cloud apps to complete tasks
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Adobe says the assistant can work across apps like Firefly, Photoshop, Premiere, Lightroom, Express, Illustrator and its other apps to do tasks for you.

item.title: This energy startup’s bet on 100-year-old grid tech is paying off
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Ayr Energy is using a shortage of power conversion equipment to break open a decades-old market.

item.title: Gitar, a startup that uses agents to secure code, emerges from stealth with $9 million
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The company uses AI to review code that, more often than not these days, has also been generated by AI.

item.title: This startup is betting tokenmaxxing will create the next compute giant
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Parasail raised $32 million in a Series A, signaling a fractured future of models and compute.

item.title: Amazon unveils a slimmer Fire TV Stick HD, opens Ember Artline TVs for preorder
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: On Wednesday, Amazon announced the launch of its new Fire TV Stick HD and Ember Artline televisions, both now available for preorder.  Following the initial release of the first-generation Fire TV Stick HD two years ago, Amazon has created a new model that is its thinnest streaming device to date, the company says. Launched in […]

item.title: Chipmakers AMD, Arm, and Qualcomm are all investing in this buzzy self-driving tech startup
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Wayve's $1.2 billion Series D round, which was announced in February, keeps growing.

item.title: HBO Max comes to India via exclusive JioHotstar deal
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: HBO Max will be available to JioHotstar subscribers as an add-on starting at ₹49 (about $0.50) per month, and would feature content from HBO, Max Originals, Warner Bros. Pictures, Warner Bros. Television, and DC Studios.

item.title: Fathom adds a bot-less meeting mode in a bid to take on Granola
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Fathom will allow you to choose between video, audio-only, and transcription modes.

item.title: Spotify launches the ability to purchase physical books in the US and UK
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Spotify users in the US and UK can officially purchase physical books through the Android app. iOS users will get the feature next week.

item.title: Anthropic’s rise is giving some OpenAI investors second thoughts
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: One investor who has backed both companies told the FT that justifying OpenAI's recent round required assuming an IPO valuation of $1.2 trillion or more — making Anthropic's current $380 billion valuation look like the relative bargain.

item.title: AI data center startup Fluidstack in talks for $1B round at $18B valuation months after hitting $7.5B, says report
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Fundraising is reportedly flowing for Fluidstack after it secured a $50 billion deal to build data centers for Anthropic.

item.title: Someone planted backdoors in dozens of WordPress plug-ins used in thousands of websites
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Dozens of WordPress plug-ins were allegedly hijacked to push malware after they were sold to a new corporate owner.

item.title: Anthropic co-founder confirms the company briefed the Trump administration on Mythos
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: In an interview at the Semafor World Economy summit this week, Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark explained why the company was still engaged with the U.S. government while simultaneously suing them.

item.title: London gets closer to its first robotaxi service as Waymo begins testing
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Waymo's commercial service will eventually follow testing if the U.K. government approves.

item.title: YouTube livestreams will now hold back ads during peak engagement to protect the vibe
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Until now, the only way to avoid seeing ads was by paying for a YouTube Premium subscription.

item.title: Max Hodak’s Science Corp. is preparing to place its first sensor in a human brain
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The device could help address multiple neurological conditions if it proves successful. One early use could be delivering gentle electrical stimulation to damaged brain or spinal cord cells to encourage healing.
